data.content:
I can see you have an MSTest project with a failing test — `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` expects `15.00` but the method returns `10.00` for express.

Let me start `dotnet watch test` so tests automatically re-run when you save changes:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to use `dotnet watch test` for hot reload. This is a .NET MSTest project targeting net8.0. I can start `dotnet watch test` which will re-run tests automatically when files change.

Let me first run the tests to see the current state, then set up hot reload with `dotnet watch test`.

data.content:
`dotnet watch test` is now running and watching for changes. Here's the current status:

- ✅ `CalculateShipping_StandardDelivery_Returns5` — passed
- ❌ `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` — **Expected `15.00`, Actual `10.00`**

The watcher will automatically rebuild and re-run tests whenever you save a file. To fix the failing test, update the express return value on line 23 from `10.00m` to `15.00m`. Want me to fix it?
